item.description: A two-person U.S. military crew was rescued after their helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz. The cause of the incident remains unknown. President Trump told reporters that “the pilots are fine,” and that no one was injured in the incident.

media:content.media:title: U.S. pilots rescued after helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A two-person U.S. military crew was rescued after their helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz. The cause of the incident remains unknown. President Trump told reporters that “the pilots are fine,” and that no one was injured in the incident.

item.title: U.S. pilots rescued after helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz

item.description: All eyes are on a key U.S. Senate race in Maine, where democratic senate hopeful Graham Platner, who has weathered a series of scandals, is likely to win a chance to unseat longtime Maine Senator Susan Collins in the general election. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Graham Platner Faces Key Test in Maine Primary
media:content.media:description: All eyes are on a key U.S. Senate race in Maine, where democratic senate hopeful Graham Platner, who has weathered a series of scandals, is likely to win a chance to unseat longtime Maine Senator Susan Collins in the general election. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: Graham Platner Faces Key Test in Maine Primary

item.description: NBC News projects that Democratic Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman will advance to a runoff with Mayor Karen Bass in the general election.

media:content.media:title: Nithya Raman to advance in L.A. mayoral runoff race, NBC News projects
media:content.media:description: NBC News projects that Democratic Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman will advance to a runoff with Mayor Karen Bass in the general election.

item.title: Nithya Raman to advance in L.A. mayoral runoff race, NBC News projects

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
Adam Edelman
item.description: Four states vote Tuesday, with Maine’s key Senate race capturing the most national attention and Trump picking sides in South Carolina’s race for governor.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner acknowledges applause at a campaign event Friday, June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine.

item.title: Graham Platner and big races for governor: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: New York Knicks fans rained vulgarities down on Victor Wembanayma and his teammates inside Madison Square Garden but the San Antonio Spurs responded with their best game of the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs v New York Knicks
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama, the San Antonio Spurs #1, celebrates during the game against the New York Knicks during Game Three of the 2026 NBA Finals on June 8, 2026 at Madison Square Garden in New York.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs send boisterous Knicks fans home quiet with Game 3 win

item.dc:creator: Jamie Knodel
Chelsea Stahl
item.description: Forget the sky. Plenty of bold-faced names are expected Monday night on Celebrity Row and throughout the crowd at Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: Celebrities and President Donald Trump attend game three of the NBA Finals in New York.
Timothée Chalamet
DJ Khaled and Fat Joe sit courtside.
Cardi B and NEw York Knicks dancers on the court.
Cardi B performs during halftime during Game Three
Spike Lee and Dave Chappelle
Celebrities sit courtside.
Larry David
President Trump reacts to the crowd
Ben Stiller kneels on the court to take photos.
Tracy Morgan riles the crowd courtside.
Chris Rock attends the game between the San Antonio Spurs against the New York Knicks.
Jay-Z and John Stewart at Madison Square Garden.
Timothee Chalamet cheers courtside.
Jeremy Lin and Spike Lee hold up peace signs.

item.title: Plenty of star power on celebrity row for Knicks-Spurs Game 3

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Raquel Coronell Uribe
Sarah Dean
item.description: President Donald Trump attended Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday to cheer on his hometown team — but many of his fellow fans didn’t appear thrilled to have him there.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Donald Trump at Madison Square Garden
media:content.media:description: U.S. President Donald Trump listens to the national anthem before the start of game 3 of the NBA Finals between New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden on June 8, 2026 in New York.

item.title: Trump booed at Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of NBA Finals

item.dc:creator: Barbara Mantel
item.description: Americans should limit their alcohol consumption to no more than one drink per day, according to a study published Tuesday in the Journal of Studies on Alcohol and Drugs

media:content.media:text: Photo Montage of Man Drinking Whiskey in the 1960's
media:content.media:description: They study found men who had more than 6.5 drinks per week and women who had more than seven per week had greater than a 1-in-1,000 lifetime risk of dying from alcohol-related diseases or injuries.

item.title: Adults should have no more than one drink a day, a team of scientists says

item.dc:creator: Tyler Kingkade
item.description: Google flew 70 teachers and administrators to its California headquarters to learn about its AI products — and how to persuade skeptical colleagues to use them.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image, on left, Winston Roberts speaks on stage, on right, a photo of a laptop screen showing a webpage with text that reads "Google AI Educator Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: An exterior view of Google's headquarters, many trees outside of a building with all glass windows
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed images, on top left, a photo of people looking down at a paper on a table, and bottom right, a hand writing a list on a large sheet of paper that reads "Distracted: gamify learning, pomodoro technique, poll about what is distracting them, activity away from devices, quick time saving demo, address distractions ahead of time (acknowledge challenges), sandbox"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Jennie Magiera sits on a chair outside
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Karen Compton leans against a glass wall
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Joseph South stands in a tree patch outside of a building
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Winston Roberts speaks on stage in front of a screen
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed photos, on the top left, a photo of someone look at a laptop screen, on the bottom right, a laptop screen with text that reads "Google Educator AI Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: A hand uses a sharpie to write "Anti-AI, remove fear" in magenta on a piece of large white paper
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

item.title: Inside Google’s AI training for teachers

item.description: Buying and selling second-hand goods at thrift stores and online marketplaces like Depop are seeing a resurgence led by young shoppers hunting for a bargain. It comes as many Americans struggle to afford the basics with inflation at a three-year high and prices rising faster than wages for the first time since 2023. NBC’s Christine Romans reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Second-Hand Stores Thrive as Affordability Becomes Top Priority
media:content.media:description: Buying and selling second-hand goods at thrift stores and online marketplaces like Depop are seeing a resurgence led by young shoppers hunting for a bargain. It comes as many Americans struggle to afford the basics with inflation at a three-year high and prices rising faster than wages for the first time since 2023. NBC’s Christine Romans reports for TODAY.

item.title: Second-Hand Stores Thrive as Affordability Becomes Top Priority

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Robinson
item.description: Four states host primary elections, including a closely watched Senate race in Maine.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, speaks at a campaign event Friday, June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine.

media:content.media:text: Steve Kornacki in a studio at NBC News.
media:content.media:description: Steve Kornacki.

media:content.media:text: Image: A slideshow gif of images from the San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks basketball game
media:content.media:description: NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks. June 8, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Hu Xiaochun guardian of the tree in Huangshan, China.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong and the 1,000-year-old tree

item.title: Primary races to watch and NBA Finals Game 3 highlights: Morning Rundown

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
Ammar Cheikh Omar
item.description: President Donald Trump insisted again Tuesday that a deal to end the war with Iran could be just days away

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-LEBANON-ISRAEL-IRAN-US-WAR
media:content.media:description: Smoke and debris rise following an Israeli airstrike in Tyre on June 9, 2026.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-LEBANON-ISRAEL-IRAN-US-WAR
media:content.media:description: The aftermath Monday of Israeli airstrikes near the archaeological site of the Roman baths in Tyre.

item.title: Israel launches new Lebanon strikes even as Trump insists Iran deal coming soon

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: The New York Knicks played in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999 and hosted President Trump. They say it’s not why they lost to the San Antonio Spurs.

media:content.media:text: New York Knicks fans wait to enter Madison Square Garden.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks fans wait to enter Madison Square Garden ahead of Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs on June 8, 2026 in New York.

item.title: Knicks say Game 3 circus wasn’t the reason for their first loss in more than a month

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: New York Knicks fans rained vulgarities down on Victor Wembanayma and his teammates inside Madison Square Garden but the San Antonio Spurs responded with their best game of the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs v New York Knicks
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama, the San Antonio Spurs #1, celebrates during the game against the New York Knicks during Game Three of the 2026 NBA Finals on June 8, 2026 at Madison Square Garden in New York.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs send boisterous Knicks fans home quiet with Game 3 win

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
Adam Edelman

media:content.media:text: A split composite image, on left, Winston Roberts speaks on stage, on right, a photo of a laptop screen showing a webpage with text that reads "Google AI Educator Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: An exterior view of Google's headquarters, many trees outside of a building with all glass windows
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed images, on top left, a photo of people looking down at a paper on a table, and bottom right, a hand writing a list on a large sheet of paper that reads "Distracted: gamify learning, pomodoro technique, poll about what is distracting them, activity away from devices, quick time saving demo, address distractions ahead of time (acknowledge challenges), sandbox"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Jennie Magiera sits on a chair outside

media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed photos, on the top left, a photo of someone look at a laptop screen, on the bottom right, a laptop screen with text that reads "Google Educator AI Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: A hand uses a sharpie to write "Anti-AI, remove fear" in magenta on a piece of large white paper
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

item.title: Inside Google’s AI training for teachers

item.dc:creator: Kayla Steinberg
item.description: Thrift shopping is back in fashion

media:content.media:text: A young girl looks through racks of clothes at a thrift store.
Marco Alvarado, store manager
media:content.media:description: Marco Alvarado, store manager at 2nd Street Montclair in Montclair, N.J., last month.

media:content.media:text: Merchandise at 2nd Street Montclair last month.
media:content.media:description: Merchandise at 2nd Street Montclair last month.

item.title: A new generation is making thrift shopping cool (again)

item.dc:creator: Barbara Mantel
item.description: Americans should limit their alcohol consumption to no more than one drink per day, according to a study published Tuesday in the Journal of Studies on Alcohol and Drugs

media:content.media:text: Photo Montage of Man Drinking Whiskey in the 1960's
media:content.media:description: They study found men who had more than 6.5 drinks per week and women who had more than seven per week had greater than a 1-in-1,000 lifetime risk of dying from alcohol-related diseases or injuries.

item.title: Adults should have no more than one drink a day, a team of scientists says

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
Courtney Kube
Mosheh Gains
Dennis Romero
item.description: A U.S. military helicopter went down near the coast of Oman and its two crew members were rescued by unmanned boats — a first for the U.S. military — Central Command said Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: An aerial view of dozens of large ships anchored in a waterway in front of tan mountains.
media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman on May 25, 2026.

item.title: Two crew members rescued after military helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz, U.S. says

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
Tara Prindiville
item.description: The Justice Department opened a separate investigation this year into the Democratic governor over allegations he helped impede immigration enforcement.

media:content.media:text: Vice President JD Vance
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance leads the White House Task Force to Eliminate Fraud.

item.title: Vance refers Walz and Minnesota attorney general to DOJ for fraud investigation

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Raquel Coronell Uribe
Sarah Dean
item.description: President Donald Trump attended Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday to cheer on his hometown team — but many of his fellow fans didn’t appear thrilled to have him there.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Donald Trump at Madison Square Garden
media:content.media:description: U.S. President Donald Trump listens to the national anthem before the start of game 3 of the NBA Finals between New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden on June 8, 2026 in New York.

item.title: Trump booed at Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of NBA Finals

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: A man wanted on suspicion of driving recklessly was attacked by an alligator but continued running from police before he was finally arrested, Louisiana authorities said Monday

media:content.media:text: An alligator is seen in a pond
media:content.media:description: An alligator in Avondale, La.

item.title: Man fleeing police attacked by alligator before continuing his getaway, Louisiana authorities say

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
Julie Tsirkin
Brennan Leach
item.description: Democrats and some Republicans were critical of the president’s defense of his “anti-weaponization” fund on NBC News’ “Meet the Press.” Others believe the idea is dead.

media:content.media:text: Protests As Joint Session Of Congress Confirms Presidential Election Result
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump has not ruled out giving taxpayer funds to rioters who fought against police officers on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Some Republicans say Trump should close the door on paying out Jan. 6 rioters
